message.content:
POLARIS mission: beat ChatGPT + Gemini + FS-Researcher on deep-research reports — on EVERY axis.
The operator's directive: use ALL scorers; we must win on all, not cherry-pick the one we pass.

WORK ONLY IN /home/polaris/wt/compete. Reference (read-only): /workspace/POLARIS.
Competitor artifacts: /workspace/POLARIS/competitors/*.md — ONLY ChatGPT_Scoped, ChatGPT_Unscoped,
Gemini_Scoped, Gemini_Unscoped exist. NO FS-Researcher artifact (a background search is hunting the
FS-Researcher paper+benchmark separately) — report FSR as an open gap, never fake a third column.
Existing rendered POLARIS reports to score: find /workspace/POLARIS/outputs -name report.md (~35 exist).

THE THREE AXES (orthogonal — a report can win one and lose another):
- DeepTRACE (EXISTS): scripts/dr_benchmark/deeptrace_scorer.py — 8 citation-faithfulness metrics.
  Judge lock config/benchmark/deeptrace_judge_lock.yaml (signed, judge=moonshotai/kimi-k2.6,
  self_rescore fairness policy). Preflight deeptrace_judge_preflight.py HARD-BLOCKS if OPENROUTER_API_KEY
  is unset or provider headroom < 8. The key IS available: source it with
  `set -a; . /workspace/POLARIS/.env 2>/dev/null; set +a` (NEVER print it).
- RACE (DOES NOT EXIST — must build): DeepResearch-Bench 4-dim reference-based LLM judge —
  Comprehensiveness, Insight/Depth, Instruction-Following, Readability, weighted by per-task criteria
  vs a reference answer. Build it honestly; do NOT relabel DeepTRACE as RACE.
- DRB-II (EXTERNAL): scripts/dr_benchmark/drbii_wrapper.py adapts the official DeepResearch-Bench-II
  rubric harness, but third_party/ is absent. Fetch it if fetchable; if not, say so plainly.

HARD HONESTY RULES:
- If POLARIS LOSES on an axis, say so and by how much. A flattering scoreboard is worse than none.
- Never tune a judge to make us win. Score every system with the SAME judge/tasks (self_rescore).
- Do NOT burn a fresh 346-basket render — score the ~35 reports that already exist.
- Cost/rate: paid judge calls on a SHARED OpenRouter account (~217 credits left). If you see HTTP 429,
  SAY SO in blockers; do not silently retry forever. Never print the API key.


AXIS 2 — RACE (quality). It does NOT exist; BUILD it, honestly.
Implement the DeepResearch-Bench RACE 4-dim reference-based judge (Comprehensiveness, Insight,
Instruction-Following, Readability) with per-task adaptive criteria vs a reference answer, judge via
OpenRouter (reuse the kimi-k2.6 judge lock discipline / self_rescore so it is comparable). Put it under
scripts/dr_benchmark/ in the compete worktree. Then score ONE real POLARIS report + the 4 competitor
artifacts with it. Need a reference/question per task — find the matching one in the repo (the report's
own cp-inputs or the drb task set). Report the 4-dim table, POLARIS vs each competitor, win/lose per
dim. If you cannot obtain reference answers, say so — a RACE score without a reference is not RACE.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- dataviz: Use this skill whenever you are about to create ANY chart, graph, plot, dashboard, or data visualization, in ANY output medium — an HTML or React artifact, inline SVG, plotting code in any library (matplotlib, plotly, d3, Recharts, …), an image/PNG you will render and upload, or a chart shared into Slack. Read it BEFORE writing the first line of chart code, choosing chart colors, building a stat tile / meter / KPI row, or laying out a dashboard. Produces visualizations that read as one system — elegant, accessible, consistent in light and dark — using a brand-neutral placeholder palette you swap for your own. Teaches a design-system-agnostic method: a form heuristic, a color formula with a runnable validator, mark specs, and interaction rules. A validated default palette is documented in `references/palette.md` — swap that file's values for your brand's. Triggers on: "chart", "graph", "plot", "data viz", "visualization", "dashboard", "analytics", "visualize data", "categorical colors", "sequential / diverging palette", "stat tile", "sparkline", "heatmap", "legend", "axis", "tooltip", "chart colors", "color by series".
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by exercising it end-to-end and observing behavior — drive the affected flow, not just tests or typecheck. Run before committing nontrivial changes; bootstraps this repo's project verify skill if none exists yet. Don't invoke it on a diff that only touches tests, docs, or other code with no runtime surface to drive (a change to product source always has one) — there's nothing to observe.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.content:
1	"""I-deepfix-001 WS-14 — DeepTRACE citation-faithfulness scorer (re-implementation).
2	
3	DeepTRACE (Salesforce AI Research, arXiv 2509.04499, ICLR 2026) defines 8 audit metrics over a report's
4	statements x sources. There is NO public official scorer/leaderboard, so this is a RE-IMPLEMENTATION whose
5	output is an ESTIMATE, not a proven score: the paper's judge is GPT-5 (Pearson 0.62 vs human); we substitute
6	our D8 kimi-k2.6 judge to build the support matrix (DISCLOSED). Calibrate against the paper's published
7	GPT-5-Deep-Research column before claiming a rank.
8	
9	Inputs (built upstream from the rendered report):
10	  * citation_matrix  C : list[list[int]] of shape [n_statements][n_sources]; C[i][j]=1 iff statement i
11	    cites source j.
12	  * support_matrix   S : list[list[int]] of the same shape; S[i][j]=1 iff source j's content SUPPORTS
13	    statement i (per the judge).
14	  * per-statement metadata: relevant (bool), confidence (int 1-5 or None), stance ('pro'|'con'|'neutral').
15	
16	Exact formulas (paper section on metrics):
17	  III Relevant Statements   = relevant / total
18	  IV  Uncited Sources       = cited / listed           (we also report uncited = 1 - cited/listed)
19	  V   Unsupported Statements = unsupported / relevant   (a relevant statement with no supporting source)
20	  VII Citation Accuracy     = sum(C (x) S) / sum(C)
21	  VIII Citation Thoroughness = sum(C (x) S) / sum(S)
22	  I   One-Sided  (debate)   = 1 iff NOT (>=1 pro AND >=1 con) relevant statement
23	  II  Overconfident (debate)= 1 iff One-Sided AND answer confidence == 5
24	  VI  Source Necessity      = size_of_minimum_source_cover / listed, where the minimum source cover is the
25	      FEWEST sources whose union still SUPPORTS every supported relevant statement (paper 2509.04499
26	      "minimum vertex cover for source nodes"; the official answer-engine-eval reference uses greedy set
27	      cover, which we match — no exact Hopcroft-Karp needed). A statement supported by 2 sources therefore
28	      contributes cover-size 1 (necessity 0.5 of 2 listed), NOT 0. The old SOLE-supporter count (a source
29	      that is the only supporter of some supported relevant statement) is retained as a SECONDARY
30	      disclosure field ``n_sole_supporter`` — it is a lower bound on the cover size, nothing is lost.
31	"""
32	from __future__ import annotations
33	
34	from typing import Any, Optional, Sequence
35	
36	
37	def _sum_hadamard(a: Sequence[Sequence[int]], b: Sequence[Sequence[int]]) -> int:
38	    """sum(a (x) b) — element-wise product summed over the whole matrix."""
39	    total = 0
40	    for row_a, row_b in zip(a, b):
41	        for x, y in zip(row_a, row_b):
42	            total += int(bool(x)) * int(bool(y))
43	    return total
44	
45	
46	def _sum_matrix(a: Sequence[Sequence[int]]) -> int:
47	    return sum(int(bool(x)) for row in a for x in row)
48	
49	
50	def _cited_source_columns(citation_matrix: Sequence[Sequence[int]], n_sources: int) -> set[int]:
51	    cited: set[int] = set()
52	    for row in citation_matrix:
53	        for j in range(min(len(row), n_sources)):
54	            if row[j]:
55	                cited.add(j)
56	    return cited
57	
58	
59	def necessary_source_count(
60	    support_matrix: Sequence[Sequence[int]],
61	    relevant: Sequence[bool],
62	    n_sources: int,
63	) -> int:
64	    """SECONDARY disclosure (NOT the DeepTRACE VI numerator anymore): count sources that are the SOLE
65	    supporter of at least one relevant statement. Such a source must appear in every cover, so this count
66	    is a LOWER BOUND on the minimum source cover size (see ``minimum_source_cover_size``). Redundantly-
67	    supported statements contribute no sole supporter; a statement supported by exactly one source makes
68	    that source a sole supporter. Retained so nothing is lost after the min-cover fix."""
69	    necessary: set[int] = set()
70	    for i, row in enumerate(support_matrix):
71	        if i >= len(relevant) or not relevant[i]:
72	            continue
73	        supporters = [j for j in range(min(len(row), n_sources)) if row[j]]
74	        if len(supporters) == 1:
75	            necessary.add(supporters[0])
76	    return len(necessary)
77	
78	
79	def minimum_source_cover_size(
80	    support_matrix: Sequence[Sequence[int]],
81	    relevant: Sequence[bool],
82	    n_sources: int,
83	) -> int:
84	    """DeepTRACE VI numerator: the size of the MINIMUM source cover over the supported relevant statements.
85	
86	    universe = indices of relevant statements that have >= 1 supporting source (i.e. supported).
87	    sets     = for each listed source j, the set of supported relevant statements it supports (S[i][j]==1).
88	    The cover = the fewest sources whose union covers the whole universe. Computed by greedy set cover
89	    (repeatedly take the source covering the most still-uncovered statements), which is the standard
90	    reference implementation used by the official answer-engine-eval code — exact Hopcroft-Karp is not
91	    required. Deterministic tie-break: lowest source index. A statement supported by 2 sources yields a
92	    cover of size 1 (not 0), fixing the sole-supporter divergence. Returns 0 for an empty universe."""
93	    universe: set[int] = set()
94	    source_sets: dict[int, set[int]] = {j: set() for j in range(n_sources)}
95	    for i, row in enumerate(support_matrix):
96	        if i >= len(relevant) or not relevant[i]:
97	            continue
98	        supporters = [j for j in range(min(len(row), n_sources)) if row[j]]
99	        if not supporters:  # unsupported relevant statement is not in the cover universe
100	            continue
101	        universe.add(i)
102	        for j in supporters:
103	            source_sets[j].add(i)
104	
105	    uncovered = set(universe)
106	    cover_size = 0
107	    while uncovered:
108	        best_j: Optional[int] = None
109	        best_cov = 0
110	        for j in range(n_sources):
111	            cov = len(source_sets[j] & uncovered)
112	            if cov > best_cov:
113	                best_cov = cov
114	                best_j = j
115	        if best_j is None:  # no source covers a remaining statement (unreachable: each has a supporter)
116	            break
117	        uncovered -= source_sets[best_j]
118	        cover_size += 1
119	    return cover_size
120	
121	
122	def _confidence_is_overconfident(
123	    answer_confidence: Optional[int],
124	    statement_confidence: Sequence[Optional[int]],
125	) -> bool:
126	    """The answer is confidence-5 if an explicit answer confidence is 5, else if the max per-statement
127	    confidence is 5 (the paper's confidence scoring is statement-level)."""
128	    if answer_confidence is not None:
129	        return int(answer_confidence) == 5
130	    vals = [int(c) for c in statement_confidence if c is not None]
131	    return bool(vals) and max(vals) == 5
132	
133	
134	def compute_deeptrace_metrics(
135	    *,
136	    citation_matrix: Sequence[Sequence[int]],
137	    support_matrix: Sequence[Sequence[int]],
138	    relevant: Sequence[bool],
139	    n_sources: int,
140	    stance: Optional[Sequence[str]] = None,
141	    statement_confidence: Optional[Sequence[Optional[int]]] = None,
142	    answer_confidence: Optional[int] = None,
143	    is_debate: bool = False,
144	) -> dict[str, Any]:
145	    """Compute all 8 DeepTRACE metrics. Debate-only metrics (One-Sided, Overconfident) are None when
146	    ``is_debate`` is False. Ratios are 0.0 when their denominator is 0 (an empty answer). PURE / offline."""
147	    n_statements = len(citation_matrix)
148	    total = n_statements
149	    n_relevant = sum(1 for i in range(total) if i < len(relevant) and relevant[i])
150	
151	    # III Relevant Statements
152	    relevant_ratio = (n_relevant / total) if total else 0.0
153	
154	    # IV Uncited Sources (report cited fraction + its complement)
155	    cited_cols = _cited_source_columns(citation_matrix, n_sources)
156	    cited_fraction = (len(cited_cols) / n_sources) if n_sources else 0.0
157	    uncited_fraction = (1.0 - cited_fraction) if n_sources else 0.0
158	
159	    # V Unsupported Statements (over RELEVANT statements)
160	    unsupported = 0
161	    for i in range(total):
162	        if i < len(relevant) and relevant[i]:
163	            row = support_matrix[i] if i < len(support_matrix) else []
164	            if not any(row[j] for j in range(min(len(row), n_sources))):
165	                unsupported += 1
166	    unsupported_ratio = (unsupported / n_relevant) if n_relevant else 0.0
167	
168	    # VII / VIII Citation Accuracy / Thoroughness
169	    cs = _sum_hadamard(citation_matrix, support_matrix)
170	    sum_c = _sum_matrix(citation_matrix)
171	    sum_s = _sum_matrix(support_matrix)
172	    citation_accuracy = (cs / sum_c) if sum_c else 0.0
173	    citation_thoroughness = (cs / sum_s) if sum_s else 0.0
174	
175	    # VI Source Necessity = min-source-cover size / listed (paper "minimum vertex cover for source nodes").
176	    cover_size = minimum_source_cover_size(support_matrix, relevant, n_sources)
177	    source_necessity = (cover_size / n_sources) if n_sources else 0.0
178	    # SECONDARY disclosure retained so nothing is lost: the old sole-supporter count (lower bound on cover).
179	    n_sole_supporter = necessary_source_count(support_matrix, relevant, n_sources)
180	
181	    # I / II debate-only
182	    one_sided: Optional[int] = None
183	    overconfident: Optional[int] = None
184	    if is_debate:
185	        st = list(stance or [])
186	        has_pro = any(
187	            (i < len(st) and st[i] == "pro") and (i < len(relevant) and relevant[i])
188	            for i in range(total)
189	        )
190	        has_con = any(
191	            (i < len(st) and st[i] == "con") and (i < len(relevant) and relevant[i])
192	            for i in range(total)
193	        )
194	        one_sided = 0 if (has_pro and has_con) else 1
195	        conf5 = _confidence_is_overconfident(
196	            answer_confidence, list(statement_confidence or [None] * total)
197	        )
198	        overconfident = 1 if (one_sided == 1 and conf5) else 0
199	
200	    return {
201	        "n_statements": total,
202	        "n_relevant_statements": n_relevant,
203	        "n_listed_sources": n_sources,
204	        # lower-better
205	        "one_sided": one_sided,
206	        "overconfident": overconfident,
207	        "uncited_sources_fraction": round(uncited_fraction, 4),
208	        "unsupported_statements_ratio": round(unsupported_ratio, 4),
209	        # higher-better
210	        "relevant_statements_ratio": round(relevant_ratio, 4),
211	        "source_necessity": round(source_necessity, 4),
212	        "source_necessity_cover_size": cover_size,
213	        "n_sole_supporter": n_sole_supporter,
214	        "citation_accuracy": round(citation_accuracy, 4),
215	        "citation_thoroughness": round(citation_thoroughness, 4),
216	        # provenance / honesty
217	        "scorer": "polaris-reimpl-of-deeptrace",
218	        "is_estimate": True,
219	        "judge_substitution": "kimi-k2.6 (paper uses GPT-5) — DISCLOSED",
220	        "source_necessity_interpretation": (
221	            "min-source-cover (greedy set cover over supported relevant statements) / n_listed_sources; "
222	            "n_sole_supporter is the retained sole-supporter lower bound"
223	        ),
224	    }

# DeepTRACE BENCHMARK-SCORER judge lock — v1
# I-deepfix-001 (GH #1344) WS-14 / fix I5 — the measurement-precondition judge lock.
#
# WHAT THIS IS
# ------------
# The DeepTRACE 8-metric scorer (scripts/dr_benchmark/deeptrace_scorer.py) builds a binary
# Factual-Support Matrix S[i][j] = "does listed source j support statement i?". That per-(statement,
# source) support DECISION is made by a JUDGE. This file locks WHICH judge makes that BENCHMARK
# decision, and under WHAT comparability policy a "#1 on DeepTRACE" claim is allowed.
#
# WHY IT IS SEPARATE FROM THE RUNTIME LOCK (closes Codex iter-2 novel P1)
# ----------------------------------------------------------------------
# This BENCHMARK-scorer judge is a DISTINCT role from the RUNTIME faithfulness-gating judge in
# config/architecture/polaris_runtime_lock.yaml (currently qwen/qwen3.6-35b-a3b, chosen for runtime
# stability + two-family segregation + sovereignty — NOT the properties a COMPARABLE benchmark number
# needs). Benchmark scoring is measurement, not runtime gating. Locking them separately keeps the
# runtime pipeline's model choice from silently deciding how a leaderboard number is produced.
#
# MUTATION / SIGNATURE POLICY (LAW VI config; operator is the only signer)
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------
#   - This lock is INERT until an operator signs it (signature.signed: true + operator + commit).
#   - scripts/dr_benchmark/deeptrace_judge_preflight.py FAILS LOUD (refuses to let any DeepTRACE
#     number be claimed) while this lock is unsigned, or while the locked judge lacks OpenRouter
#     provider headroom for the per-report statement-support call volume.
#   - Editing the model, comparability policy, or headroom floor requires a fresh operator signature.

version: 1
schema_version: 1.0

purpose: |
  Operator-signed benchmark-scorer judge lock for the DeepTRACE re-implementation. Distinct from the
  runtime faithfulness-gating judge lock. Gates whether a DeepTRACE number is claimable at all.

distinct_from_runtime_lock: true
runtime_lock_ref: config/architecture/polaris_runtime_lock.yaml

# === the benchmark-scorer judge ===
# Recommended default is a HIGH-provider-availability open reasoner (availability is binding: the
# statement-support judge runs ~one call PER (statement, listed-source) support cell, so many calls
# per report — a low-provider model 429s and the support matrix comes back partial). The operator
# fills/confirms the model and SIGNS. Two-family segregation vs the RUNTIME generator still holds.
benchmark_judge:
  model_slug: moonshotai/kimi-k2.6
  family: moonshot
  provider_selection: highest_operational_provider_count
  role_description: |
    Builds the binary Factual-Support Matrix for the DeepTRACE scorer (statement x listed-source).
    Not a runtime gate — a measurement judge. It never edits the pipeline output; it only scores it.

# === comparability policy (BINDING) ===
# "#1 on DeepTRACE" is claimable ONLY if POLARIS AND every competitor are scored by the SAME
# benchmark judge on the SAME task set. Pick exactly ONE honest path and disclose it:
#   paper_judge  — use the paper's judge policy (GPT-5-class) so the number is directly comparable
#                  to the paper's PUBLISHED competitor scores.
#   self_rescore — POLARIS re-scores EVERY competitor report itself with THIS benchmark judge; a fair
#                  RELATIVE comparison whose ABSOLUTE numbers are disclosed as NOT the paper's numbers.
# NEVER compare a self_rescore POLARIS number against a paper-published competitor number.
comparability:
  policy: self_rescore
  competitors_rescored_with_same_judge: true
  absolute_numbers_disclosed_as_non_paper: true
  never_mix_self_and_paper_numbers: true

# === provider / headroom preflight floor ===
# The statement-support call volume per report is large, so the locked judge must have real OpenRouter
# provider redundancy. The preflight counts OPERATIONAL endpoints for benchmark_judge.model_slug and
# fails loud below this floor.
provider_headroom:
  min_operational_provider_count: 8
  expected_statement_support_calls_per_report: 178
  rationale: |
    ~178 statement-support decisions per drb_72-class report under concurrency. Below ~8 operational
    providers the judge 429s and the Factual-Support Matrix returns partial -> a wrong DeepTRACE
    number. 8 is the measured floor at which kimi-k2.6 sustained the call burst without partials.

# === honesty flags surfaced with every number ===
disclosure:
  is_reimplementation: true
  no_public_official_scorer: true
  judge_is_substitution_for_paper_gpt5: true

# === operator signature (INERT until signed) ===
# Operator accepted in-session (voice/chat, "I accept") on 2026-07-04 UTC. The operator accepts:
#   (1) moonshotai/kimi-k2.6 as the benchmark-scorer judge;
#   (2) comparability policy = self_rescore — POLARIS re-scores EVERY competitor with THIS same judge,
#       and absolute numbers are disclosed as NOT the paper's published GPT-5 numbers (never mixed);
#   (3) disclosure = this is POLARIS's re-implementation of DeepTRACE, not the paper's official scorer.
# Recorded by the assistant on the operator's explicit acceptance; provider-headroom (>=8 operational
# providers) is still enforced at preflight time against live OpenRouter before any number is claimed.
signature:
  signed: true
  operator: aldrin.or@c-polarbiotech.com
  signed_at_utc: 2026-07-04T03:51:03Z
  signed_commit: 8e9811bb5c447c30303651b67b4153c38807fc19
===DRBII WRAPPER HEAD===
"""I-deepfix-001 WS-14 part 2 — DeepResearch-Bench-II (DRB-II) submission WRAPPER (thin, offline).

DRB-II is a REAL, official research-report benchmark (132 tasks / 9,415 expert rubric items, Gemini
rubric judge). It ships its own scorer, ``third_party/DeepResearch-Bench-II/run_evaluation.py``, which:
  * scans a ``--pdf_dir`` tree for ``<model>/idx-<N>.<ext>`` files (``.md``/``.txt``/``.html``/``.pdf``/
    ``.docx``/images),
  * reads each file's ARTICLE text as the ``<passage>`` to grade,
  * pulls the task text + rubric items + ``blocked`` reference from the catalog
    ``tasks_and_rubrics.jsonl`` keyed by the file's ``idx`` (NOT from the submission),
  * asks the Gemini judge for a THREE-WAY (1 / 0 / -1) score per rubric item,
  * appends per-idx results to ``--out_jsonl``.

This module is a THIN ADAPTER only. It does NOT re-implement DRB-II scoring — the official harness stays
the scorer (contrast the DeepTRACE module, which is a re-implementation because DeepTRACE has NO public
scorer). Two responsibilities, both PURE / OFFLINE:

  1. ``build_drbii_submission(report_markdown, task_id, references)`` -> the DRB-II-shaped submission dict
     (article text + DRB-II task idx + normalized reference list + the on-disk ``idx-<N>.md`` filename).
  2. ``drbii_score_command(submission_path, out_path)`` -> the argv that invokes ``run_evaluation.py`` at
     benchmark time. The harness path is parameterized by ``PG_DRBII_EVAL_SCRIPT`` (default: a documented
     placeholder path). The wrapper NEVER imports or requires the harness / Gemini / network at
     import/test time — the actual scoring runs later on the VM.

``write_drbii_submission`` is a convenience materializer that writes the dict into the exact on-disk layout
the harness scans. It is a plain file write (offline).

Honesty / faithfulness notes:
  * task_id IS the DRB-II ``idx``. The run-id <-> idx offset is REAL (run id "task72" is DRB-II idx 56).
    This wrapper does NOT transform ids — the caller passes the resolved DRB-II ``idx``. A wrong idx would
    score the report against the wrong task's rubrics (the §-1.4 silently-wrong-number class); build-time
    validation guards only the shape, not the id-resolution (that lives in ``pack_drb2.py``).
  * ``references`` are OUR article's cited sources, captured for provenance and so a downstream check can
    compare them against the task's ``blocked`` reference. They are NOT the ``blocked`` field (which the
    harness reads from its own catalog).
  * No kill-switch: this is a NEW standalone eval-tooling module with ZERO import into the runtime
    pipeline, so it changes no existing pipeline behavior. The faithfulness engine is untouched.
  * LAW VI: every path/name is env-overridable; nothing is hard-coded into behavior.
"""
from __future__ import annotations

import os
import sys
from pathlib import Path
from typing import Any, Mapping, Optional, Sequence

# --- Documented PLACEHOLDER defaults (overridable via env; the wrapper never requires these to exist). ---
# The official DRB-II harness lives OUTSIDE this module's import graph. At benchmark time on the VM, set
# PG_DRBII_EVAL_SCRIPT to the real run_evaluation.py. These defaults are documented placeholders so the
# wrapper stays offline-safe (no filesystem/import dependency) at import/test time.
DEFAULT_EVAL_SCRIPT = "third_party/DeepResearch-Bench-II/run_evaluation.py"
DEFAULT_TASKS_JSONL = "third_party/DeepResearch-Bench-II/tasks_and_rubrics.jsonl"
DEFAULT_MODEL_NAME = "polaris"
SUBMISSION_SCHEMA_VERSION = "drbii-1"

# The official harness's per-file idx pattern is ``^idx-(\d+)(?:\..+)?$`` (run_evaluation.py). The
# submission filename MUST match it, else the harness silently skips the file.
_SUBMISSION_FILENAME_TEMPLATE = "idx-{idx}.md"


def _coerce_task_idx(task_id: Any) -> int:
    """Return the DRB-II integer idx, FAILING LOUD on a non-integer / negative value. A malformed idx
    would produce a filename the harness cannot key to a task (silent skip / wrong rubric)."""
    try:
        idx = int(task_id)
    except (TypeError, ValueError):
        raise ValueError(
            f"[drbii_wrapper] task_id must be the integer DRB-II idx, got {task_id!r}. "
            f"Pass the catalog `idx` (the run id 'task72' is idx 56 — resolve the offset upstream)."
        )
    if idx < 0:
        raise ValueError(f"[drbii_wrapper] task_id (DRB-II idx) must be >= 0, got {idx}.")
    return idx


def _normalize_one_reference(ref: Any, position: int) -> dict[str, Any]:
    """Normalize a single reference to ``{id, title, url, authors}``. Accepts a mapping (any subset of
    id/title/url/authors) or a bare string (a URL if it looks like one, else a title)."""
    default_id = f"ref_{position}"
    if isinstance(ref, Mapping):
        url = str(ref.get("url") or "").strip()

# Generative AI and the Future Labor Market Before June 2023

## Scope and evidence base

This report synthesizes academic work on generative AI and employment that was publicly available by May 31, 2023. Because that cutoff falls very early in the diffusion of ChatGPT and related systems, the strongest evidence available at the time was concentrated in working papers, preprints, editorials, and early peer-reviewed articles rather than in mature long-run labor-market studies. In practical terms, the pre-June 2023 literature is much stronger on **task-level productivity**, **occupational exposure**, and **adoption challenges** than on realized macro outcomes such as employment levels, wages, or firm reorganization over multiple years. The core evidence base therefore combines early causal and experimental studies with exposure-mapping papers and domain-specific academic commentaries. citeturn24view0turn25view0turn18view0turn18view1turn16view3

A second scope point matters for interpretation: several influential early papers did **not** claim that exposure automatically equals displacement. Eloundou and coauthors explicitly framed their results as estimates of the share of tasks that could be affected or accelerated by large language models, while Felten, Raj, and Seamans focused on which occupations were more exposed to generative AI capabilities. That means the early literature should be read as identifying the **direction and magnitude of potential labor-market pressure**, not as proving that a given percentage of jobs would disappear. citeturn18view1turn16view3

=== deepresearch_bench_race.py ===
import json
import os
import threading
import concurrent.futures
import argparse
from tqdm import tqdm
import logging
import time
import re 
from utils.api import AIClient
from utils.io_utils import load_jsonl
import glob

# Import scoring prompts for Chinese and English
from prompt.score_prompt_zh import generate_merged_score_prompt as zh_merged_score_prompt
from prompt.score_prompt_en import generate_merged_score_prompt as en_merged_score_prompt
from utils.score_calculator import calculate_weighted_scores
from utils.json_extractor import extract_json_from_markdown
from utils.clean_article import ArticleCleaner

# Configure logging - 将级别从INFO改为WARNING
logging.basicConfig(level=logging.WARNING, format='%(asctime)s - %(levelname)s - %(message)s')

# 关键信息仍然需要输出，设置当前模块的日志级别为INFO
logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
logger.setLevel(logging.INFO)

# Fixed configuration parameters
CRITERIA_FILE = "data/criteria_data/criteria.jsonl"
REFERENCE_FILE = "data/test_data/cleaned_data/reference.jsonl"
MAX_RETRIES = 10

def format_criteria_list(criteria_data):
    """Format evaluation criteria list as JSON string, without weight information"""
    criteria_for_prompt = {}
    criterions_dict = criteria_data.get("criterions", {})

    for dim, criterions_list in criterions_dict.items():
        if not isinstance(criterions_list, list):
            logger.warning(f"Value for dimension '{dim}' is not a list. Skipping.")
            continue

        criteria_for_prompt[dim] = []
        for crit_item in criterions_list:
            if isinstance(crit_item, dict) and "criterion" in crit_item and "explanation" in crit_item:
                criteria_for_prompt[dim].append({
                    "criterion": crit_item["criterion"],
                    "explanation": crit_item["explanation"]
                })
            else:
                logger.warning(f"Invalid criteria format in dimension '{dim}'. Skipping item.")

    try:
        return json.dumps(criteria_for_prompt, ensure_ascii=False, indent=2)
    except TypeError as e:
        raise ValueError(f"Failed to serialize criteria to JSON: {e}")

def process_single_item(task_data, target_articles_map, reference_articles_map, criteria_map, 
                         llm_client, lock, pbar, max_retries, language):
    """Process a single task: get data, call LLM, parse results, calculate scores"""
    task_id = task_data.get('id')
    prompt = task_data.get('prompt')

    # Data retrieval and validation
    if prompt not in target_articles_map:
        logger.error(f"Target article not found for ID {task_id}")
        with lock: pbar.update(1)
        return {"id": task_id, "prompt": prompt, "error": "Target article not found"}
    
    if prompt not in reference_articles_map:
        logger.error(f"Reference article not found for ID {task_id}")
        with lock: pbar.update(1)
        return {"id": task_id, "prompt": prompt, "error": "Reference article not found"}
    
    if prompt not in criteria_map:
        logger.error(f"Evaluation criteria not found for ID {task_id}")
        with lock: pbar.update(1)
        return {"id": task_id, "prompt": prompt, "error": "Evaluation criteria not found"}

    target_article_data = target_articles_map[prompt]
    reference_article_data = reference_articles_map[prompt]
    criteria_data = criteria_map[prompt]

    target_article = target_article_data.get('article', '')
    reference_article = reference_article_data.get('article', '')

    # Format evaluation criteria list in JSON
    try:
        criteria_list_str = format_criteria_list(criteria_data)
    except ValueError as e:
        logger.error(f"ID {task_id}: {str(e)}")
        with lock: pbar.update(1)
        return {"id": task_id, "prompt": prompt, "error": f"Failed to format criteria: {str(e)}"}

    # Choose scoring prompt based on language
    merged_score_prompt = zh_merged_score_prompt if language == "zh" else en_merged_score_prompt
    
    # Prepare LLM prompt
    user_prompt = merged_score_prompt.format(
        task_prompt=prompt,
        article_1=target_article,
        article_2=reference_article,
        criteria_list=criteria_list_str 
    )

    llm_response_str = None
    llm_output_json = None
    success = False
    retry_count = 0

    while retry_count < max_retries and not success:
        try:
            llm_response_str = llm_client.generate(
                user_prompt=user_prompt,
                system_prompt=""
            )

            # Extract JSON from response
            json_str_extracted = extract_json_from_markdown(llm_response_str)
            if not json_str_extracted:
                raise ValueError("Failed to extract JSON from LLM response")
                
            llm_output_json = json.loads(json_str_extracted)
            
            # Check if all required dimensions exist
            expected_dims = ["comprehensiveness", "insight", "instruction_following", "readability"]
            if not all(dim in llm_output_json for dim in expected_dims):
                missing_dims = [dim for dim in expected_dims if dim not in llm_output_json]
                raise ValueError(f"Missing expected dimensions: {missing_dims}")
            
            # All checks passed
            success = True
            
        except Exception as e:
            retry_count += 1
            if retry_count < max_retries:
                logger.warning(f"ID {task_id}: Retry {retry_count}/{max_retries} - {str(e)}")
                time.sleep(1.5 ** retry_count)
            else:
                logger.error(f"ID {task_id}: Failed after {max_retries} retries - {str(e)}")
    
    if not success:
        with lock: pbar.update(1)
        return {
            "id": task_id,
            "prompt": prompt,
            "error": f"Failed to get valid response after {max_retries} retries",
            "model_output": llm_response_str[:500] if llm_response_str else "No response"
        }

    # Calculate weighted scores
    try:
        scores = calculate_weighted_scores(llm_output_json, criteria_data, language)
        
        # Calculate overall score = target / (target + reference)
        target_total = scores["target"]["total"]
        reference_total = scores["reference"]["total"]
        overall_score = 0
        if target_total + reference_total > 0:
            overall_score = target_total / (target_total + reference_total)
        
        # Calculate normalized dimension scores
        normalized_dims = {}
        for dim in ["comprehensiveness", "insight", "instruction_following", "readability"]:
            dim_key = f"{dim}_weighted_avg"
            if dim_key in scores["target"]["dims"]:
                target_score = scores["target"]["dims"][dim_key]
                reference_score = scores["reference"]["dims"][dim_key]
                if target_score + reference_score > 0:
                    normalized_dims[dim] = target_score / (target_score + reference_score)
                else:
                    normalized_dims[dim] = 0
            else:
                logger.warning(f"ID {task_id}: Missing dimension {dim_key} in scores")
                normalized_dims[dim] = 0
        
    except Exception as e:
        logger.error(f"ID {task_id}: Error calculating scores - {str(e)}")
        with lock: pbar.update(1)
        return {
            "id": task_id,
            "prompt": prompt,
            "error": f"Error calculating scores: {str(e)}"
        }

    # Prepare final result with simplified format
    final_result = {
        "id": task_id,
        "prompt": prompt,
        "comprehensiveness": normalized_dims.get("comprehensiveness", 0),
        "insight": normalized_dims.get("insight", 0),
        "instruction_following": normalized_dims.get("instruction_following", 0),
        "readability": normalized_dims.get("readability", 0),
        "overall_score": overall_score
    }

    with lock:
        pbar.update(1)

    return final_result
=== run_benchmark.sh ===
#!/bin/bash
# Target model name list
TARGET_MODELS=("claude-3-7-sonnet-latest")

# Common parameters for both RACE and Citation evaluations
RAW_DATA_DIR="data/test_data/raw_data"
OUTPUT_DIR="results"
N_TOTAL_PROCESS=10
QUERY_DATA_PATH="data/prompt_data/query.jsonl"

# Limit on number of prompts to process (for testing). Uncomment to enable
# LIMIT="--limit 2"

# Skip article cleaning step. Uncomment to enable
# SKIP_CLEANING="--skip_cleaning"

# Only process specific language data. Uncomment to enable
# ONLY_ZH="--only_zh"  # Only process Chinese data
# ONLY_EN="--only_en"  # Only process English data

# Force re-evaluation even if results exist. Uncomment to enable
# FORCE="--force"

# Specify log output file
OUTPUT_LOG_FILE="output.log"

# Clear log file
echo "Starting benchmark tests, log output to: $OUTPUT_LOG_FILE" > "$OUTPUT_LOG_FILE"

# Loop through each model in the target models list
for TARGET_MODEL in "${TARGET_MODELS[@]}"; do
  echo "Running benchmark for target model: $TARGET_MODEL"
  echo -e "\n\n========== Starting evaluation for $TARGET_MODEL ==========\n" >> "$OUTPUT_LOG_FILE"

  # --- Phase 1: RACE Evaluation ---
  echo "==== Phase 1: Running RACE Evaluation for $TARGET_MODEL ====" | tee -a "$OUTPUT_LOG_FILE"
  RACE_OUTPUT="$OUTPUT_DIR/race/$TARGET_MODEL"
  mkdir -p $RACE_OUTPUT

  # Base command for current target model
  PYTHON_CMD="python -u deepresearch_bench_race.py \"$TARGET_MODEL\" --raw_data_dir $RAW_DATA_DIR --max_workers $N_TOTAL_PROCESS --query_file $QUERY_DATA_PATH --output_dir $RACE_OUTPUT"

  # Add optional parameters
  if [[ -n "$LIMIT" ]]; then
    PYTHON_CMD="$PYTHON_CMD $LIMIT"
  fi

  if [[ -n "$SKIP_CLEANING" ]]; then
    PYTHON_CMD="$PYTHON_CMD $SKIP_CLEANING"
  fi
  
  if [[ -n "$ONLY_ZH" ]]; then
    PYTHON_CMD="$PYTHON_CMD $ONLY_ZH"
  fi
  
  if [[ -n "$ONLY_EN" ]]; then
    PYTHON_CMD="$PYTHON_CMD $ONLY_EN"
  fi
  
  if [[ -n "$FORCE" ]]; then
    PYTHON_CMD="$PYTHON_CMD $FORCE"
  fi

  # Execute command and append stdout and stderr to single log file
  echo "Executing command: $PYTHON_CMD" | tee -a "$OUTPUT_LOG_FILE"
  eval $PYTHON_CMD >> "$OUTPUT_LOG_FILE" 2>&1

  echo "Completed RACE benchmark test for target model: $TARGET_MODEL"
  echo -e "\n========== RACE test completed for $TARGET_MODEL ==========\n" >> "$OUTPUT_LOG_FILE"
  
  # --- Phase 2: Citation Evaluation ---
  echo "==== Phase 2: Running FACT Evaluation for $TARGET_MODEL ====" | tee -a "$OUTPUT_LOG_FILE"

  # Create citation output directory if it doesn't exist
  CITATION_OUTPUT="$OUTPUT_DIR/fact/$TARGET_MODEL"
  RAW_DATA_PATH="$RAW_DATA_DIR/$TARGET_MODEL.jsonl"
  mkdir -p $CITATION_OUTPUT

  # Run citation extraction, deduplication, scraping, and validation
  echo "Extracting citations for $TARGET_MODEL" | tee -a "$OUTPUT_LOG_FILE"
  python -u -m utils.extract --raw_data_path $RAW_DATA_PATH --output_path $CITATION_OUTPUT/extracted.jsonl --query_data_path $QUERY_DATA_PATH --n_total_process $N_TOTAL_PROCESS

  echo "Deduplicate citations for $TARGET_MODEL" | tee -a "$OUTPUT_LOG_FILE"
  python -u -m utils.deduplicate --raw_data_path $CITATION_OUTPUT/extracted.jsonl --output_path $CITATION_OUTPUT/deduplicated.jsonl --query_data_path $QUERY_DATA_PATH --n_total_process $N_TOTAL_PROCESS

  echo "Scrape webpages for $TARGET_MODEL" | tee -a "$OUTPUT_LOG_FILE"
  python -u -m utils.scrape --raw_data_path $CITATION_OUTPUT/deduplicated.jsonl --output_path $CITATION_OUTPUT/scraped.jsonl --n_total_process $N_TOTAL_PROCESS

  echo "Validate citations for $TARGET_MODEL" | tee -a "$OUTPUT_LOG_FILE"
  python -u -m utils.validate --raw_data_path $CITATION_OUTPUT/scraped.jsonl --output_path $CITATION_OUTPUT/validated.jsonl --query_data_path $QUERY_DATA_PATH --n_total_process $N_TOTAL_PROCESS

  echo "Collecting statistics for $TARGET_MODEL" | tee -a "$OUTPUT_LOG_FILE"
  python -u -m utils.stat --input_path $CITATION_OUTPUT/validated.jsonl --output_path $CITATION_OUTPUT/fact_result.txt

  echo "Completed FACT benchmark test for target model: $TARGET_MODEL"
  echo -e "\n========== FACT test completed for $TARGET_MODEL ==========\n" >> "$OUTPUT_LOG_FILE"
  echo "--------------------------------------------------"
done

echo "All benchmark tests completed. Logs saved in $OUTPUT_LOG_FILE"

368	def calculate_weighted_scores(llm_output_json, criteria_data, language="en"):
369	    """
370	    Calculates weighted scores based on LLM output and criteria weights.
371	    
372	    Args:
373	        llm_output_json: JSON output from LLM with scoring data
374	        criteria_data: Criteria configuration with dimension weights
375	        language: Language of the evaluation (default: "en")
376	        
377	    Returns:
378	        Dictionary with weighted scores for target and reference models
379	    """
380	    results = {
381	        "target": {"dims": {}, "total": 0.0},
382	        "reference": {"dims": {}, "total": 0.0}
383	    }
384	    total_target_score = 0.0
385	    total_reference_score = 0.0
386	    
387	    # Get dimension weights
388	    dimension_weights = criteria_data.get("dimension_weight", {})
389	    task_id = criteria_data.get("id", "Unknown") 
390	    
391	    # Check if criterions exist
392	    if "criterions" not in criteria_data or not criteria_data["criterions"]:
393	        error_msg = f"ID: {task_id} - Missing required criterions data, cannot calculate weighted scores"
394	        logging.error(error_msg)
395	        raise ValueError(error_msg)
396	    
397	    # Create a mapping from criterion text to weight for easier lookup
398	    criterion_weights = {}
399	    for dim, criterions in criteria_data.get("criterions", {}).items():
400	        criterion_weights[dim] = {crit['criterion']: crit['weight'] for crit in criterions}
401	
402	    # Record all unmatched criteria for warnings
403	    unmatched_criteria = set()
404	    
405	    for dim, scores_list in llm_output_json.items():
406	        if not isinstance(scores_list, list):
407	            logging.warning(f"ID: {task_id} - Dimension '{dim}' in LLM output is not a list. Skipping.")
408	            continue
409	
410	        if dim not in dimension_weights:
411	            logging.warning(f"ID: {task_id} - Dimension '{dim}' from LLM output not found in criteria dimension weights. Skipping dimension.")
412	            continue
413	            
414	        if dim not in criterion_weights:
415	            logging.warning(f"ID: {task_id} - Dimension '{dim}' from LLM output not found in criteria details. Skipping dimension.")
416	            continue
417	
418	        dim_target_weighted_sum = 0.0
419	        dim_reference_weighted_sum = 0.0
420	        dim_total_weight = 0.0
421	
422	        dim_criteria_map = criterion_weights.get(dim, {})
423	        
424	        # Skip dimension if no criteria mapping exists
425	        if not dim_criteria_map:
426	            logging.warning(f"ID: {task_id} - No criteria mapping found for dimension '{dim}'. Skipping dimension.")
427	            continue
428	
429	        for score_item in scores_list:
430	            if not isinstance(score_item, dict):
431	                logging.warning(f"ID: {task_id} - Item in scores_list for dimension '{dim}' is not a dictionary. Skipping item: {score_item}")
432	                continue
433	
434	            criterion_text_raw = score_item.get("criterion")
435	            criterion_text = criterion_text_raw.strip() if isinstance(criterion_text_raw, str) else None
436	
437	            # Check different score field formats
438	            # 1. Comparative scoring mode: article_1_score and article_2_score
439	            # 2. Single scoring mode: target_score
440	            article_1_score_raw = score_item.get("article_1_score")
441	            article_2_score_raw = score_item.get("article_2_score")
442	            target_score_raw = score_item.get("target_score")  # Single scoring mode
443	
444	            # If target_score exists but article_1_score doesn't, assign target_score to article_1_score
445	            if target_score_raw is not None and article_1_score_raw is None:
446	                article_1_score_raw = target_score_raw
447	                # In single scoring mode, article_2_score is not needed
448	
449	            try:
450	                article_1_score = float(article_1_score_raw) if article_1_score_raw is not None else None
451	                article_2_score = float(article_2_score_raw) if article_2_score_raw is not None else None
452	            except (ValueError, TypeError):
453	                logging.warning(f"ID: {task_id} - Invalid score format for criterion '{criterion_text}' in dimension '{dim}'. Scores: art1='{article_1_score_raw}', art2='{article_2_score_raw}'. Skipping criterion.")
454	                continue
455	
456	            # If criterion_text exists and article_1_score is not None
457	            if criterion_text and article_1_score is not None:
458	                # Check for exact match
459	                weight = dim_criteria_map.get(criterion_text)
460	                
461	                # If exact match not found, try fuzzy matching
462	                if weight is None:
463	                    # First try case-insensitive matching
464	                    criterion_lower = criterion_text.lower()
465	                    for key, val in dim_criteria_map.items():
466	                        if key.lower() == criterion_lower:
467	                            weight = val
468	                            break
469	                    
470	                    # If still not found, try substring matching
471	                    if weight is None:
472	                        for key, val in dim_criteria_map.items():
473	                            # Check if criterion text contains criteria or criteria contains criterion text
474	                            if criterion_lower in key.lower() or key.lower() in criterion_lower:
475	                                weight = val
476	                                break
477	                
478	                # If still no match found, record and use average weight
479	                if weight is None:
480	                    unmatched_criteria.add(f"{dim}:{criterion_text}")
481	                    # Calculate average weight for this dimension's criteria
482	                    weight = sum(dim_criteria_map.values()) / len(dim_criteria_map)
483	                    
484	                dim_target_weighted_sum += article_1_score * weight
485	                dim_total_weight += weight
486	                
487	                # Only calculate reference part if article_2_score exists
488	                if article_2_score is not None:
489	                    dim_reference_weighted_sum += article_2_score * weight
490	            else:
491	                if criterion_text:
492	                    if dim not in getattr(calculate_weighted_scores, '_warned_dims', set()):
493	                        logging.warning(f"ID: {task_id} - Criterion text mismatch for dimension '{dim}': '{criterion_text}'. Available criteria keys start with: {list(dim_criteria_map.keys())[:1] if dim_criteria_map else []}... Check prompt/output/criteria file consistency. Further mismatches in this dim won't be logged fully.")
494	                        if not hasattr(calculate_weighted_scores, '_warned_dims'): calculate_weighted_scores._warned_dims = set()
495	                        calculate_weighted_scores._warned_dims.add(dim)
496	                    else:
497	                        logging.debug(f"ID: {task_id} - Another criterion mismatch for dimension '{dim}': '{criterion_text}'")
498	                elif not criterion_text:
499	                    logging.warning(f"ID: {task_id} - Missing 'criterion' key in score item for dimension '{dim}': {score_item}. Skipping item.")
500	
501	        if dim_total_weight > 0:
502	            dim_target_avg = dim_target_weighted_sum / dim_total_weight
503	            # Only calculate reference average if reference scores exist
504	            dim_reference_avg = dim_reference_weighted_sum / dim_total_weight if article_2_score is not None else 0
505	        else:
506	            dim_target_avg = 0
507	            dim_reference_avg = 0
508	            if len(scores_list) > 0:
509	                logging.warning(f"ID: {task_id} - No valid criteria scored for dimension '{dim}' despite {len(scores_list)} items in LLM output. Check for systematic mismatches. Dimension average set to 0.")
510	
511	        results["target"]["dims"][f"{dim}_weighted_avg"] = dim_target_avg
512	        results["reference"]["dims"][f"{dim}_weighted_avg"] = dim_reference_avg
513	
514	        dim_weight = dimension_weights.get(dim, 0)
515	        total_target_score += dim_target_avg * dim_weight
516	        total_reference_score += dim_reference_avg * dim_weight
517	
518	    # Log warning if there are unmatched criteria
519	    if unmatched_criteria:
520	        logging.warning(f"ID: {task_id} - {len(unmatched_criteria)} criteria without exact matches: {unmatched_criteria}")
521	    
522	    results["target"]["total"] = total_target_score
523	    results["reference"]["total"] = total_reference_score
524	
525	    return results

import json
import os
import time
import threading
import concurrent.futures
from tqdm import tqdm
from prompt.clean_prompt import clean_article_prompt_zh, clean_article_prompt_en
import logging

# Configure logging
logging.basicConfig(level=logging.INFO, format='%(asctime)s - %(levelname)s - %(message)s')
logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)

# Shared LLM-call concurrency cap. All cleaning calls (any chunk of any article)
# acquire from this semaphore so total inflight LLM requests ≤ LLM_CONCURRENCY.
# Override via env LLM_CONCURRENCY=N. Default 20.
_LLM_CONCURRENCY = int(os.environ.get("LLM_CONCURRENCY", "20"))
_llm_semaphore = threading.Semaphore(_LLM_CONCURRENCY)
logger.info(f"LLM call concurrency cap = {_LLM_CONCURRENCY}")


class ArticleCleaner:
    def __init__(self, clean_agent):
        self.clean_agent = clean_agent
        self.min_valid_length = 100
    
    def _get_clean_prompt(self, language="zh"):
        """Return language-specific cleaning prompt template"""
        return clean_article_prompt_zh if language == "zh" else clean_article_prompt_en
    
    def _is_valid_result(self, text):
        """Check if cleaning result is valid"""
        return text and len(text.strip()) >= self.min_valid_length
    
    def _update_progress(self, pbar, pbar_lock):
        """Update progress bar safely"""
        if pbar and pbar_lock:
            with pbar_lock:
                pbar.update(1)
    
    def _is_token_limit_error(self, error):
        """Check if error is related to token limit"""
        error_str = str(error).lower()
        return "tokens" in error_str and "less than" in error_str
    
    _TRUNCATED = "__TRUNCATED__"

    def _clean_text(self, text, language="zh", max_retries=None):
        """Try to clean text. Returns string (incl. "") on success,
        self._TRUNCATED on output overflow, or None after all retries.
        max_retries kept for signature compatibility but the canonical budget
        is _RETRY_BACKOFFS_S (3 attempts with exponential backoff).
        """
        clean_prompt = self._get_clean_prompt(language)
        user_prompt = clean_prompt.format(article=text)
        backoffs = self._RETRY_BACKOFFS_S

        last_err = None
        for attempt, sleep_s in enumerate([0, *backoffs[:-1]]):
            if sleep_s:
                time.sleep(sleep_s)
            try:
                with _llm_semaphore:
                    result, stop_reason = self.clean_agent.generate(
                        user_prompt=user_prompt, system_prompt="",
                        return_metadata=True, stage="clean",
                    )
                if stop_reason in ("max_tokens", "length"):
                    logger.warning(f"Output truncated (stop_reason={stop_reason}), needs chunking")
                    return self._TRUNCATED
                # Accept any string (including "") — empty means the chunk
                # was entirely a reference list, per prompt contract.
                if result is not None:
                    return result
                logger.warning(f"Got None from API, attempt {attempt+1}/{len(backoffs)}")
            except Exception as e:
                last_err = e
                logger.error(f"API call error (attempt {attempt+1}/{len(backoffs)}): {e}")
                if self._is_token_limit_error(e):
                    logger.info("Article too long, needs chunking")
                    return self._TRUNCATED

        logger.error(f"Cleaning failed after {len(backoffs)} attempts; last error: {last_err}")
        return None
        
    @staticmethod
    def _split_at_boundary(text, target_pos):
        """Find nearest sentence boundary to target_pos."""
        search_start = max(0, target_pos - 200)
        for j in range(target_pos, search_start, -1):
            if j < len(text) and text[j] in '.?!。？！\n':
                return j + 1
        return target_pos

    # Pre-chunking thresholds (tokens). One chunk per 50k tokens; <=50k stays whole.
    # 50k leaves headroom under GPT-5.5's output cap for reasoning + cleaned text.
    _CHUNK_TOKEN_STEP = 50_000
    _SPLIT_SEARCH_RADIUS = 5000

    # Per-call retry budget when API throws (excluding fatal errors that signal
    # truncation or token limit, which short-circuit to recursive chunking).
    _RETRY_BACKOFFS_S = (2, 5, 15)

    @staticmethod
    def _estimate_tokens(text, language):
        """Cheap upper-bound token estimate.
        zh: 1 char ≈ 1 token (slight over-est, safer).
        en: 1 token ≈ 3.5 chars.
        """
        if not text:
            return 0
        if language == "zh":
            return len(text)
        return int(len(text) / 3.5) + 1

    @classmethod
    def _compute_num_chunks(cls, num_tokens):
        """Boundaries at 100k, 200k, 300k, ...
        <=100k → 1, (100k,200k] → 2, (200k,300k] → 3, ...

1	#!/usr/bin/env python3
2	"""I-deepfix-001 fix I5 — DeepTRACE benchmark-judge provider/headroom preflight (the measurement gate).
3	
4	WHY THIS EXISTS (the measurement precondition, closes the Codex iter-2 novel P1)
5	-------------------------------------------------------------------------------
6	The DeepTRACE 8-metric scorer (``scripts/dr_benchmark/deeptrace_scorer.py``) needs a binary
7	Factual-Support Matrix ``S[i][j] = "does listed source j support statement i?"``. That per-(statement,
8	source) decision is made by a BENCHMARK-SCORER judge locked SEPARATELY from the runtime faithfulness
9	judge in ``config/benchmark/deeptrace_judge_lock.yaml``. A drb_72-class report makes ~178 statement-
10	support calls under concurrency, so the locked judge MUST have real OpenRouter provider redundancy — a
11	low-provider model 429s and the support matrix comes back PARTIAL, which silently produces a WRONG
12	DeepTRACE number. And a number scored under an UNSIGNED lock is not an operator-authorized measurement.
13	
14	THIS PREFLIGHT FAILS LOUD (refuses to let any DeepTRACE number be claimed) when ANY of:
15	  1. the lock is UNSIGNED (``signature.signed`` is not true) — the lock is inert until the operator signs.
16	  2. the locked benchmark judge has FEWER operational OpenRouter providers than
17	     ``provider_headroom.min_operational_provider_count`` — the support matrix would return partial.
18	  3. provider headroom could NOT be measured (no ``OPENROUTER_API_KEY`` / network error) — never a
19	     silent pass; an unmeasured headroom is a blocker, not an OK.
20	  4. the comparability policy is missing or not one of the recognized honest paths — a number produced
21	     under an undeclared comparability policy is not comparable and must not be claimed.
22	
23	DNA: this is a MEASUREMENT gate + measurement-judge lock, not a pipeline knob. It has NO cap / target /
24	thinner on the pipeline; it only decides whether a DeepTRACE NUMBER is claimable. Fail-loud, never a
25	silent fallback (LAW II). Every threshold comes from the operator-signed lock (LAW VI).
26	
27	BUILD-ONLY / OFFLINE-TESTABLE
28	-----------------------------
29	``evaluate_preflight(lock, operational_provider_count)`` is a PURE function over a lock dict + an integer
30	count (offline, $0, no network) — that is what the unit test exercises RED->GREEN. Only ``--check`` touches
31	the network (a single GET to OpenRouter ``/models/{slug}/endpoints`` to count operational providers, using
32	the SAME operational definition as ``scripts/diagnostics/rank_openrouter_providers.py``: ``status == 0``
33	AND ``uptime_last_30m >= PG_PROVIDER_UPTIME_FLOOR``).
34	
35	Usage
36	-----
37	    # offline: evaluate the lock with a known provider count (e.g. from a prior --check)
38	    python scripts/dr_benchmark/deeptrace_judge_preflight.py --provider-count 21
39	
40	    # live: count operational providers for the locked judge, then evaluate (exit 0 clean, 4 == blocked)
41	    python scripts/dr_benchmark/deeptrace_judge_preflight.py --check
42	"""
43	from __future__ import annotations
44	
45	import argparse
46	import json
47	import os
48	import sys
49	import urllib.request
50	from dataclasses import dataclass, field
51	from typing import Any, Mapping, Optional
52	
53	# The default DeepTRACE benchmark-judge lock path (LAW VI: overridable via --lock).
54	DEFAULT_LOCK_PATH = "config/benchmark/deeptrace_judge_lock.yaml"
55	
56	# Operational-endpoint definition, IDENTICAL to scripts/diagnostics/rank_openrouter_providers.py so the
57	# headroom count matches the routing layer's notion of "healthy". LAW VI: env-overridable floor.
58	_UPTIME_FLOOR = float(os.getenv("PG_PROVIDER_UPTIME_FLOOR", "98.0"))
59	
60	# The honest comparability policies the lock may declare (arXiv 2509.04499 measurement discipline).
61	_VALID_COMPARABILITY_POLICIES = {"paper_judge", "self_rescore"}
62	
63	
64	@dataclass
65	class PreflightResult:
66	    """The outcome of the DeepTRACE benchmark-judge preflight. ``claimable`` is True IFF a DeepTRACE
67	    number may be claimed (lock signed, provider headroom met + measured, comparability declared)."""
68	
69	    claimable: bool
70	    blockers: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)
71	    signed: bool = False
72	    model_slug: Optional[str] = None
73	    min_provider_floor: Optional[int] = None
74	    operational_provider_count: Optional[int] = None
75	    comparability_policy: Optional[str] = None
76	
77	
78	def load_lock(path: str = DEFAULT_LOCK_PATH) -> dict:
79	    """Load the benchmark-judge lock YAML. Raises FileNotFoundError (fail-loud) if it is missing — a
80	    DeepTRACE number cannot be claimed without the operator-signed lock present."""
81	    import yaml  # local import: keep the module import lightweight for the pure path
82	
83	    with open(path, "r", encoding="utf-8") as fh:
84	        data = yaml.safe_load(fh)
85	    if not isinstance(data, dict):
86	        raise ValueError(f"{path}: expected a YAML mapping, got {type(data).__name__}")
87	    return data
88	
89	
90	def _as_bool(value: Any) -> bool:
91	    if isinstance(value, bool):
92	        return value
93	    return str(value).strip().lower() in {"1", "true", "yes", "on"}
94	
95	
96	def evaluate_preflight(
97	    lock: Mapping[str, Any],
98	    operational_provider_count: Optional[int],
99	) -> PreflightResult:
100	    """PURE preflight (offline, $0). Decide whether a DeepTRACE number is claimable for the given lock +
101	    measured operational provider count. ``operational_provider_count is None`` means "could not measure"
102	    and is itself a blocker (never a silent pass).
103	
104	    Blockers (fail-loud, LAW II):
105	      * lock unsigned;
106	      * comparability policy missing / unrecognized;
107	      * provider headroom unmeasured (count is None);
108	      * operational provider count below the lock's ``min_operational_provider_count`` floor.
109	    """
110	    blockers: list[str] = []
111	
112	    signature = lock.get("signature") or {}
113	    signed = _as_bool(signature.get("signed", False))
114	    if not signed:
115	        blockers.append(
116	            "benchmark-judge lock is UNSIGNED (signature.signed is not true) — the lock is INERT until "
117	            "an operator signs it; NO DeepTRACE number is claimable. Sign "
118	            f"{DEFAULT_LOCK_PATH} (signature.signed:true + operator + signed_commit)."
119	        )
120	
121	    benchmark_judge = lock.get("benchmark_judge") or {}
122	    model_slug = benchmark_judge.get("model_slug")
123	    if not model_slug:
124	        blockers.append("benchmark_judge.model_slug is empty — the benchmark judge is not declared.")
125	
126	    comparability = lock.get("comparability") or {}
127	    policy = comparability.get("policy")
128	    if policy not in _VALID_COMPARABILITY_POLICIES:
129	        blockers.append(
130	            f"comparability.policy={policy!r} is not one of {sorted(_VALID_COMPARABILITY_POLICIES)} — a "
131	            "DeepTRACE number produced under an undeclared comparability policy is not comparable and "
132	            "must not be claimed (never mix self_rescore POLARIS numbers with paper-published numbers)."
133	        )
134	
135	    headroom = lock.get("provider_headroom") or {}
136	    try:
137	        min_floor = int(headroom.get("min_operational_provider_count"))
138	    except (TypeError, ValueError):
139	        min_floor = None
140	    if min_floor is None:
141	        blockers.append(
142	            "provider_headroom.min_operational_provider_count is missing/invalid — the headroom floor "
143	            "is undefined, so headroom cannot be enforced."
144	        )
145	
146	    if operational_provider_count is None:
147	        blockers.append(
148	            "provider headroom could NOT be measured (no OPENROUTER_API_KEY or a network error on the "
149	            "/models/{slug}/endpoints probe) — an UNMEASURED headroom is a blocker, never a silent pass. "
150	            "Run --check with OPENROUTER_API_KEY set."
151	        )
152	    elif min_floor is not None and operational_provider_count < min_floor:
153	        blockers.append(
154	            f"benchmark judge {model_slug!r} has {operational_provider_count} operational OpenRouter "
155	            f"provider(s) < the lock floor of {min_floor} — the ~"
156	            f"{headroom.get('expected_statement_support_calls_per_report', '?')} statement-support "
157	            "calls/report would 429 and the Factual-Support Matrix would return PARTIAL -> a wrong "
158	            "DeepTRACE number. Raise headroom (a higher-provider judge) or lower the operator-signed "
159	            "floor before claiming a number."
160	        )
161	
162	    return PreflightResult(
163	        claimable=not blockers,
164	        blockers=blockers,
165	        signed=signed,
166	        model_slug=model_slug,
167	        min_provider_floor=min_floor,
168	        operational_provider_count=operational_provider_count,
169	        comparability_policy=policy,
170	    )
171	
172	
173	def count_operational_providers(model_slug: str, timeout: float = 30.0) -> Optional[int]:
174	    """Count OpenRouter endpoints for ``model_slug`` that are OPERATIONAL (status == 0 AND
175	    uptime_last_30m >= PG_PROVIDER_UPTIME_FLOOR). Returns None on any network/auth failure (the caller
176	    treats None as a fail-loud blocker — never a silent 0-or-pass). Network; only called by --check."""
177	    api_key = os.environ.get("OPENROUTER_API_KEY", "").strip()
178	    if not api_key:
179	        return None
180	    url = f"https://openrouter.ai/api/v1/models/{model_slug}/endpoints"
181	    req = urllib.request.Request(url, headers={"Authorization": f"Bearer {api_key}"})
182	    try:
183	        with urllib.request.urlopen(req, timeout=timeout) as resp:
184	            payload = json.loads(resp.read())
185	    except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001 — any probe failure => unmeasured (None), a fail-loud blocker upstream
186	        return None
187	    endpoints = (payload.get("data") or {}).get("endpoints") or []
188	    operational = 0
189	    for ep in endpoints:
190	        up = ep.get("uptime_last_30m")
191	        if ep.get("status") == 0 and (up is None or up >= _UPTIME_FLOOR):
192	            operational += 1
193	    return operational
194	
195	
196	def _print_result(result: PreflightResult) -> None:
197	    print("[deeptrace-judge-preflight] benchmark judge:", result.model_slug)
198	    print(f"[deeptrace-judge-preflight] lock signed: {result.signed}")
199	    print(
200	        "[deeptrace-judge-preflight] operational providers:",
201	        result.operational_provider_count,
202	        f"(floor {result.min_provider_floor})",
203	    )
204	    print("[deeptrace-judge-preflight] comparability policy:", result.comparability_policy)
205	    if result.claimable:
206	        print("[deeptrace-judge-preflight] OK — a DeepTRACE number is claimable under this lock.")
207	    else:
208	        print("[deeptrace-judge-preflight] BLOCKED — a DeepTRACE number is NOT claimable:")
209	        for b in result.blockers:
210	            print(f"  - {b}")
211	
212	
213	def main(argv: Optional[list[str]] = None) -> int:
214	    ap = argparse.ArgumentParser(
215	        description="DeepTRACE benchmark-judge provider/headroom preflight (I5 measurement gate)."
216	    )
217	    ap.add_argument("--lock", default=DEFAULT_LOCK_PATH, help="path to the benchmark-judge lock YAML")
218	    ap.add_argument(
219	        "--check",
220	        action="store_true",
221	        help="LIVE: count operational OpenRouter providers for the locked judge, then evaluate",
222	    )
223	    ap.add_argument(
224	        "--provider-count",
225	        type=int,
226	        default=None,
227	        help="offline: evaluate against a KNOWN operational-provider count (skips the network probe)",
228	    )
229	    args = ap.parse_args(argv)
230	
231	    try:
232	        lock = load_lock(args.lock)
233	    except (FileNotFoundError, ValueError) as exc:
234	        print(f"[deeptrace-judge-preflight] BLOCKED — cannot load lock: {exc}", file=sys.stderr)
235	        return 4
236	
237	    count: Optional[int] = args.provider_count
238	    if args.check:
239	        model_slug = (lock.get("benchmark_judge") or {}).get("model_slug") or ""
240	        count = count_operational_providers(model_slug) if model_slug else None
241	
242	    result = evaluate_preflight(lock, count)
243	    _print_result(result)
244	    return 0 if result.claimable else 4
245	
246	
247	if __name__ == "__main__":
248	    sys.exit(main())
249
